channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Isabela City Hall Employee Killed in Ambush Amid Series of Recent Attacks
item.description: ZAMBOANGA CITY, Philippines – A tragic incident unfolded in Isabela City, Basilan, as Carliecel Nuena, a 53-year-old utility worker at the city mayor's office, was shot dead while riding her motorcycl...

item.title: Bharat Electronics Limited reports 16% growth in operational revenue for FY 2025-26
item.description: Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL), the government-owned defence PSU, has reported a strong performance in the financial year 2025-26. The company recorded revenue from operations of ₹27,479.63 crore, m...

item.title: EPIC Report Highlights Failures in Data Brokers and AI Firms’ Opt-Out Processes
item.description: A recent report by the Electronic Privacy Information Center (EPIC) reveals that 38 major data-collecting companies in the U.S.—including AI vendors, data brokers, defense contractors, and dating apps...

item.title: Jennifer Aniston’s 1997 Tatler Magazine Photoshoot by Matthew Rolston
item.description: In August 1997, Jennifer Aniston appeared in the British edition of Tatler Magazine during a pivotal time in her career. At the height of her fame from the hit TV show Friends, where she portrayed the...

item.title: South Africa launches twice-yearly HIV prevention injection, but challenges remain
item.description: South Africa is set to roll out lenacapavir (LEN), a groundbreaking HIV prevention injection, starting June 5 in Mpumalanga. Taken just twice a year, LEN offers a highly effective alternative to daily...

item.title: Supreme Court Rejects Bato Dela Rosa’s Request Against ICC Warrant; Pia Cayetano Leads Senate Panel
item.description: On May 20, 2026, the Supreme Court of the Philippines officially rejected Senator Bato Dela Rosa’s plea for a temporary restraining order (TRO) against an International Criminal Court (ICC) arrest war...

item.title: Supreme Court Reviews Bail of Accused in Rawalpindi Honour Killing Case
item.description: The Supreme Court of Pakistan has issued notices to the accused involved in a Rawalpindi honour killing, where a young woman named Sidra was murdered after a local jirga rejected her choice of spouse....

item.title: Federal High Court fixes July 15 for NBF president’s fraud arraignment
item.description: The Federal High Court for Abuja don fix 15th of July make Azania Omo-Agege, the interim president of Nigeria Boxing Federation (NBF), go enter court for arraignment over N40 million fraud allegations...

item.title: Researchers Identify Enzyme IDOL as a Potential Target for Alzheimer’s Treatment
item.description: Researchers at Indiana University School of Medicine have identified an enzyme called IDOL as a potential new target for treating Alzheimer's disease. Laboratory experiments demonstrated that removing...

item.title: MGK criticises Yungblud over ticket prices and past tour cancellation in social media dispute
item.description: Machine Gun Kelly has publicly criticised fellow musician Yungblud, calling him a “silver spooned preachy wanker” and telling him to “shut the fuck up” in a now-deleted social media post. The exchange...

item.title: NAHCON launches cloud call centre to support Nigerian pilgrims during Hajj
item.description: The National Hajj Commission of Nigeria (NAHCON) don deploy new cloud-based Call Centre system to make sure say Nigerian pilgrims get better welfare services for the 2026 Hajj inside Saudi Arabia. For...

item.title: Spiritual Leader Aga Khan V Receives Warm Welcome in Islamabad
item.description: Prince Rahim Aga Khan V, the esteemed spiritual leader of the Ismaili community, arrived in Islamabad on Wednesday. He was received by President Asif Ali Zardari at Nur Khan Airbase, accompanied by Fi...

item.title: Closing arguments in the trial of Dean Penney for the death of his wife
item.description: The first-degree murder trial of Dean Penney, a man from St. Anthony, Newfoundland and Labrador, has reached its closing arguments. The Crown alleges that Penney killed his estranged wife, Jennifer Hi...

item.title: Review: Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed is a tense and inventive thriller with strong performances
item.description: Apple TV's 10-part series 'Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed' is a gripping thriller that blends dark comedy with suspense. The story centres on Paula, a newly divorced mother played brilliantly by Tatiana ...

item.title: Government pilots new approach to support workers instead of issuing fit notes
item.description: The UK government has launched a pilot scheme aimed at reforming the use of GP fit notes, which declare people unable to work. Under the new initiative, GPs in certain areas will either provide fit no...

item.title: Ruth K Voices Confidence in Arsenal’s Chances to Win UEFA Champions League
item.description: Kenyan content creator Ruth K has publicly expressed strong belief that Arsenal Football Club is capable of winning the UEFA Champions League. Following Arsenal’s historic victory in the 2025/26 Engli...

item.title: Human Rights Lawyer Petitions AU Over Rising Xenophobic Violence in South Africa
item.description: Human rights lawyer Femi Falana has formally petitioned the African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights (ACHPR) regarding the ongoing xenophobic attacks in South Africa, urging that they be recogn...

item.title: Trump Supports Flavored Vapes Amid Political and Industry Interests
item.description: Former President Donald Trump has taken steps to expand access to flavored e-cigarettes, signaling strong support for the vaping community. Earlier in his administration, Trump initially planned to ba...

item.title: Behind the Scenes of the 1975 Epic The Man Who Would Be King Starring Sean Connery and Michael Caine
item.description: The Man Who Would Be King is a classic 1975 epic adventure film directed by John Huston, adapted from Rudyard Kipling’s 1888 novella. The movie stars Sean Connery as Daniel Dravot and Michael Caine as...

item.title: Families Advocate for Support Workers to Accompany Patients in Newfoundland and Labrador Hospitals
item.description: Families in Newfoundland and Labrador are raising concerns about patients being admitted to acute care without their trusted support workers. Many patients, including those with intellectual or develo...
